name: Eggnog Chiffon Pie
recipeIngredient: 1 cup Pillsbury's Best All Purpose Flour, sifted
recipeInstructions:
1. Sift together flour and salt into a mixing bowl.
2. Cut in shortening until particles are the size of small peas.
3. Sprinkle with 3 to 4 tablespoons of cold water over mixture while tossing and stirring lightly with a fork.
4. Add water to driest particles, pushing lumps to side, until dough is just moist enough to hold together.
5. Form into ball. Flatten to 1/2-inch thickness; smooth edges.
6. Roll out on floured surface to a circle 1 1/2 inches larger than inverted 9-inch pie pan. Fit loosely into pie pan.
7. Fold edge to form a standing rim; flute.
8. Bake in hot oven (450 degrees) for 10 to 12 minutes. Cool.
9. In a saucepan, combine gelatin, sugar, and salt.
10. Add egg yolks and 1 1/4 cups milk. Cook over low heat, stirring constantly, until gelatin dissolves and mixture coats a metal spoon.
11. Chill until thickened but not set.
12. Beat egg whites until soft mounds form. Gradually add 1/4 cup sugar, beating until stiff peaks form.
13. Set aside.
14. Beat whipping cream with vanilla, rum extract, and nutmeg until thick.
15. Fold into gelatin mixture.
16. Fold in beaten egg whites gently but thoroughly.
17. Spoon lightly into baked pie shell.
18. Chill until firm, at least 2 hours. If desired, sprinkle with additional nutmeg.
